evidence: ExplorationHandlers.java imports six inner classes from SemanticFieldExplorer directly (ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ExploreOptions); TODO comment acknowledges this as a binding problem ('Once SEF inner classes are moved to a dedicated model/ package, update these imports').

handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti in ExplorationHandlers bypasses SemanticFieldExplorer entirely, calling executor.executeSurfacePattern directly and manually computing set intersections — duplicating orchestration logic that semanticFieldExplorer.compareSeeds() or a similar method should own.

All three exploration handler methods (handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticField) build response Maps<String,Object> inline with repetitive Math.round(...*100.0)/100.0 rounding, no shared DTO or serialization helper.

WordSketchApiServer constructor creates SemanticFieldExplorer with 'new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor)' rather than receiving it via injection, partially undermining the DI pattern already used for executor and grammarConfig.

executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, headPosConstraint, minLogDice, maxResults) exists as a public method on BlackLabQueryExecutor but is absent from the QueryExecutor interface — callers of the interface cannot access it, and Main.handleBlackLabQueryCommand casts to BlackLabQueryExecutor to work around this.

BlackLabSnippetParser sits at the seam between API/query and provides both XML parsing, plain-text extraction, pattern-label analysis (findLabelPosition), and sentence trimming — mixed responsibilities that blur where each concern belongs.
HttpApiUtils.sendJsonResponse and sendError provide a clean, consistent HTTP output seam used uniformly by all handlers.
QueryExecutor interface has clear, well-documented method contracts differentiating findCollocations vs executeSurfacePattern vs executeBcqlQuery.
CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens is extracted as a shared primitive used by both PatternSubstitution (api) and GrammarConfigLoader (config), avoiding duplication across the API/config seam.
GrammarConfigLoader is cleanly isolated in config; handlers receive it via constructor injection and interact through getRelation() / getRelations() — no internal state leakage.

strengths: HttpApiUtils centralizes all HTTP response logic (sendJsonResponse, sendError, sendOptionsResponse, requireMethod) — callers never touch raw exchange mechanics.
QueryExecutor interface clearly documents the distinctions between its four query methods; the boundary between API and query layers is well-specified.
GrammarConfigLoader is received via constructor injection in all handlers, keeping the grammar-loading concern isolated in the config package.
CqlUtils.splitCqlTokens is properly extracted as a shared cross-layer primitive rather than duplicated inline.
resolveRelationAlias in ExplorationHandlers centralizes front-end alias translation in one method, avoiding scattered switch logic.
mid_level_elegance.issue_character: The dominant pattern is seam bleed: inner result classes from a query-layer class (SemanticFieldExplorer) are imported directly into the API layer, multi-seed orchestration logic is partially duplicated inside a handler rather than delegated to the explorer, and response serialization is embedded inline rather than at a dedicated translation boundary.
mid_level_elegance.score_rationale: The codebase clears a basic functionality bar — the QueryExecutor interface and HttpApiUtils provide two genuinely clean seams — but multiple handoff points are compromised. API handlers import query-layer inner classes (acknowledged TODO), one handler completely bypasses the semantic explorer for multi-seed logic, and response mapping is scattered across three large methods rather than a shared translation layer. These are recurring patterns at the API↔query boundary, not isolated incidents, placing the codebase in the lower half of the mid-tier range rather than approaching a well-factored score.

issues.summary: API layer imports six SemanticFieldExplorer inner classes, coupling api to query internals
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.java lines 10-16: 'import pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult; import ...AdjectiveProfile; import ...ExplorationResult; import ...DiscoveredNoun; import ...CoreCollocate; import ...ExploreOptions;'
TODO(42e2a9a1) acknowledges: 'These imports bind the API layer to SEF's inner result classes. Once SEF inner classes are moved to a dedicated model/ package, update these imports.'
issues.suggestion: Move ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExploreOptions, and Edge to a new pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model package. Update all imports in ExplorationHandlers and SemanticFieldExplorer. This removes the direct api→query.SemanticFieldExplorer inner-class dependency.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti duplicates multi-seed orchestration instead of delegating to SemanticFieldExplorer
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti calls executor.executeSurfacePattern directly in a loop, computes set intersection with commonCollocates.retainAll(seedCollocates), and builds edges manually — logic that belongs in SemanticFieldExplorer.
SemanticFieldExplorer already has compareSeeds() and compare() methods for multi-seed comparison; handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti ignores them.
issues.suggestion: Add a SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMulti(Set<String> seeds, String bcqlPatternTemplate, ExploreOptions opts) method that encapsulates the per-seed loop, intersection computation, and edge building. ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti should call this single method and then map the result to a response map.

issues.summary: Three handler methods build response Map<String,Object> inline with duplicated rounding and field-naming logic
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, and handleSemanticField each contain 40-60 lines of 'Map<String,Object> xMap = new HashMap<>(); xMap.put(...)' with identical Math.round(x*100.0)/100.0 rounding patterns for logDice, similarity_score, avg_logdice etc.
No shared DTO or toMap() helper between the three methods.
issues.suggestion: Introduce private static toCollocateMap(WordSketchResult), toDiscoveredNounMap(DiscoveredNoun), toCoreCollocateMap(CoreCollocate), and toEdgeMap(Edge) helper methods (or static factory on the DTOs once they are in model/). This removes the inline serialization noise and makes boundary translation explicit.

issues.summary: executeDependencyPattern is public on BlackLabQueryExecutor but absent from QueryExecutor interface
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor exposes executeDependencyPattern(lemma, deprel, headPosConstraint, minLogDice, maxResults) as public but QueryExecutor only declares findDependencyCollocations(lemma, deprel, minLogDice, maxResults).
The richer signature (with headPosConstraint) is inaccessible through the interface, forcing callers to downcast or use the concrete type.
issues.suggestion: Either add executeDependencyPattern to QueryExecutor with a default implementation that ignores headPosConstraint, or consolidate into findDependencyCollocations with an optional constraint parameter. Document the chosen boundary contract on the interface.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer creates SemanticFieldExplorer internally instead of receiving it via DI
evidence: WordSketchApiServer constructor: 'SemanticFieldExplorer semanticFieldExplorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor);' — constructed inside, not passed in.
TODO(595bb84c) acknowledges construction complexity but does not address the DI gap for SemanticFieldExplorer.
issues.suggestion: Add SemanticFieldExplorer as a constructor parameter to WordSketchApiServer. Update Main.handleServerCommand to construct SemanticFieldExplorer and pass it in. This keeps the construction boundary at Main and makes WordSketchApiServer purely compositional.

root_causes: No model/DTO layer was created early enough; result types accumulated as inner classes inside SemanticFieldExplorer and are now referenced across the api↔query boundary.
Multi-seed handler was written directly against the executor rather than extending the SemanticFieldExplorer, leaving orchestration logic split between two places.
likely_symptoms: sef-inner-class-leak is a symptom of the missing model layer root cause.
inline-response-serialization is a symptom of the same missing DTO layer.
